puzzle.title: The Outcast from the Garden
puzzle.intro: A mysterious being once dwelled in the highest ranks of creation, closer to the Divine than many could fathom. Yet pride took root, and a single act of defiance altered the course of existence. Now, this being wanders as a sworn enemy, whispering and scheming until a promised day arrives. Can you uncover the identity of this fallen one?
clues: I was once among those closest to the Divine presence, elevated above much of creation.
I was created from a scorching element, not from earth or clay.
When commanded to bow before a new creation, I refused — driven by what I believed was my own superiority.
I was cast out and cursed, but ultimately granted a reprieve until a known, appointed time.
I vowed to misguide as many as I could, approaching the offspring of Adam from every direction.
I am the open, declared enemy of all of humanity, ever plotting and whispering until the appointed end.

trial.label: Trial / Event
options: Building a golden calf for the people to worship
Teaching sorcery to the people of Babylon
Splitting the sea with a staff
Carrying the Queen of Sheba's throne in an instant

outcome.label: Outcome
options: Respite granted until the Day of Judgment
Drowned in the sea with an army
Buried alive under a collapsing palace
Transformed into an ape or monkey
outcome.answer: Respite granted until the Day of Judgment

puzzle.arabic: قَالَ رَبِّ بِمَآ أَغْوَيْتَنِى لَأُزَيِّنَنَّ لَهُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَأُغْوِيَنَّهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ
puzzle.verse: [Iblees] said, "My Lord, because You have put me in error, I will surely make [disobedience] attractive to them [i.e., mankind] on earth, and I will mislead them all (15:39)
